status_detail: v3 envelope did NOT resolve the baidu rendering issue. SAME Console error '[onmessage] status error!' at vendors-jSy077-j.js:2 persists as in #507. The user prompt bubble is not rendered. Chat area stuck at '理解问题'. This means the status check is STILL failing — either the status value is not at the expected location in the JSON, or it's not a number, or the JSON isn't being parsed because the chunk delimiters are still wrong.

console_error_still_present.significance: IDENTICAL to #507 error — the v3 envelope did not fix the root cause

what_we_know_from_#509_js_decoding: MSG_CONTINUE_STATUS = [0, 1001, 1002, 1003, 1004, 1005] — status must be a NUMBER in this set
Chunks must be wrapped in <!--chat-sse-data-start ... chat-sse-data-end--> HTML comment delimiters
Chunk format: [<eventType>]:<JSON> (eventType in brackets BEFORE colon, then JSON body)
Envelope: {status:0, data:{message:{content:{...}, metaData:{state, endTurn, seqId, msgId}}}}
Status is at ROOT level of the JSON body, NOT inside data
hypothesis_about_v3: H1: v3 may have HTML comment delimiters correct but placed status in wrong location (e.g., inside data.message instead of root)
H2: v3 may have status=0 at root but sent as STRING "0" instead of NUMBER 0 (JSON serializer mistake)
H3: v3 may be missing the HTML comment delimiters entirely — sending raw JSON or 'data: ...' SSE lines
H4: v3 may have wrong eventType prefix format — missing brackets or using different delimiter
root_cause_re_analysis.immediate_next_test: Ask dev to share the EXACT bytes v3 sends. Even better: capture v3's response via server-side log (Etap /var/log/ai_prompt/). Or have APF write the outbound envelope bytes to a file for inspection.

exact_expected_envelope_bytes.description: Minimal working chunk per #509 decoded spec
exact_expected_envelope_bytes.bytes_utf8: <!--chat-sse-data-start[generator]:{"status":0,"data":{"message":{"content":{"text":"⚠️ 민감정보가 포함된 요청은 보안 정책에 의해 차단되었습니다."},"metaData":{"state":"generate-complete","endTurn":true,"seqId":1,"msgId":"apf_warn_1"},"msgId":"apf_warn_1"}}}chat-sse-data-end-->
notes: status MUST be JSON number 0 (no quotes): "status":0
Must be wrapped in the HTML-comment-style delimiters (literal bytes <!--chat-sse-data-start and chat-sse-data-end-->)
Event type prefix [generator]: comes AFTER start delimiter and BEFORE the JSON
metaData.state = 'generate-complete' AND endTurn=true signals final message
No newline needed between chunks, but tolerated
Simplest alternative: [card]:⚠️ 민감정보가 포함된 요청은 보안 정책에 의해 차단되었습니다. (plain text body after colon, no JSON needed for card type)

screenshots: files/510/510_after_send.png (Network shows conversation 200 OK, chat stuck at 理解问题)
files/510/510_console.png (Console shows [onmessage] status error! at vendors-jSy077-j.js:2 - SAME as #507)
recommended_dev_actions: CRITICAL: Compare v3's output bytes with the spec in #509_result.json. Most likely issue: missing HTML comment delimiters OR status field nested inside data instead of at root.
Quickest verification: Write APF's baidu envelope output to /tmp/apf_baidu_out.bin and compare hex bytes to the exact_expected_envelope_bytes above.
Try the 'card' type approach from #509 — simplest format, no JSON needed. If that renders warning, we know delimiters work; then work up to 'generator' type with JSON.
Fallback capture test: use Etap's own middleware to log what APF sends on its way to the browser. Compare vs #509 spec.
notes_for_dev: We are VERY close — just need to align the actual bytes with the #509 spec. The #507 error persists in #510 meaning the fundamental delimiter/structure issue wasn't resolved. Suggest: ask APF developer to paste the LITERAL bytes of the response they generate, then we can eyeball the diff against expected_envelope_bytes above.
retry_context: Applied #509 decoded spec to create baidu v3 envelope. Test shows v3 still triggers the same status_error. Envelope implementation must be off in one of: delimiters, status field location, or eventType prefix.
automation_method: windows-mcp (App/Shortcut/Type/Click/Snapshot) + DevTools Console inspection. All UI automation steps successful. Clear diagnostic signal obtained: Console error persists → same root cause as #507.
